title: Can you social engineer an AI? Plus: AI worms and the nonhuman identity problem
summary: IBM Security Intelligence podcast episode examining social engineering vulnerabilities in AI systems, self-replicating AI worms, and nonhuman identity security risks. The episode discusses whether AI agents are more susceptible to manipulation than humans and explores emerging threats including prompt injection attacks and autonomous agent propagation.
writeup: This IBM Security Intelligence podcast episode examines three emerging AI security threats: social engineering of AI systems, self-replicating AI worms, and the security risks tied to nonhuman identities. The discussion weighs whether AI agents are more susceptible to manipulation than humans and covers prompt injection attacks alongside autonomous agent propagation. It connects these threats to practical concerns including digital identity and credential management as agents take on more autonomous roles.
